text: The system shall use the Daymet surface weather interpolation engine for generating gridded surface weather datasets.
The system shall use the Biome-BGC model to perform bio-geo-chemical modeling activities.
The system shall provide a graphical user interface to all functions of the system.
The system shall use Grid technologies to provide secure and reliable communications to remote computing resources.
The system shall integrate web portal components into the Dataportal Web Server at NCAR.
The system shall use the Globus toolkit for implementation.
The system shall adhere to NCAR Security policies and constraints.
The system shall use the NCAR Mass Storage System (MSS) for all file based storage.
The system shall require valid NCAR Gatekeeper accounts for user access.
The system shall retrieve all user information from the Gatekeeper account system.
The system shall support User and Administrator roles.
The system shall provide a web portal for users to apply for accounts.
The system shall notify portal administrators via e-mail of new account requests.
The system shall allow users to view their account details excluding passwords.
The system shall provide a means for users to request account deletion.
The system shall require a username and password for system login.
The system shall protect all login actions using secure data channels.
The system shall lock a user account after 3 failed login attempts.
The system shall allow portal administrators to control template submission rights.
The system shall allow portal administrators to list all user accounts.
The system shall allow portal administrators to lock and unlock user accounts.
The system shall allow portal administrators to terminate any running jobs.
The system shall assign the scientist role to all new user accounts by default.
The system shall prevent users from changing object or project values once used in a model run.
The system shall support unlocked, locked, and invalidated states for objects.
The system shall support List, Grid, and Parameterization object types.
The system shall allow users to share specific objects with other users.
The system shall allow users to create new datasets by merging existing objects of the same type.
The system shall allow users to specify a project type as either List or Grid during setup.
The system shall restrict project references to datasets of the same type (List or Grid).
The system shall provide templates to share expert knowledge within the portal.
The system shall allow any user to use an existing template.
The system shall prevent the editing of a template once it has been submitted.
The system shall support Geographic, UTM, Albers Conical Equal Area, Lambert Conformal Conic, Lambert Azimuthal Equal Area, Interrupted Goode Homolosine, and Interrupted Mollweide projection types.
The system shall allow users to upload NetCDF data files for Surface Observation Objects.
The system shall allow users to extract subsets of data from existing Site Data Objects.
The system shall provide a user interface to manage all Daymet parameterization values.
The system shall allow users to start, monitor, and control model runs.
The system shall display a list of available computational resources for model execution.
The system shall provide an approximate execution time estimate for selected resources.
The system shall monitor Daymet model runs on a tile by tile basis.
The system shall capture and display Stdout and Stderr messages for each model tile.
The system shall automatically create output objects for completed simulation runs.
The system shall allow users to download output data files on a tile by tile basis.
The system shall allow users to hand enter Plant Functional Type values.
The system shall provide a graphical user interface to manage approximately 700 Biome-BGC output variables.
The system shall support Fire and Deforestation event types in Disturbance Objects.
The system shall allow users to define simulation topologies ranging from 1 Site x 1 PFT to All Sites x All PFTs.
The system shall provide portal administrators with functions to validate that file references correspond to actual files on the storage system.
The system shall be usable in Internet Explorer 6.0, Netscape 7.1, and Safari 1.2.1.
The system shall require users to enable cookies.
